title: Contributing to estatr
text: Thanks taking time contribute!

title: Contributing to estatr
text: Never commit appId. Tests run entirely offline mocked responses; need real e-Stat key work package get CI passing. record fixtures live API, redactor tests/testthat/setup.R scrubs appId — verify committing. Keep package internals dependency-lean: data.table hot paths, httr2 HTTP, rlang/cli conditions messaging. dplyr/purrr vignette examples , never package code. Use base pipe (|>) base regex magrittr/stringr. Every exported function validates arguments hitting network surfaces e-Stat errors classed conditions, raw HTTP errors.

title: Contributing to estatr
code: # Load the package for interactive work pkgload::load_all()  # Run tests (offline, no key required) devtools::test()  # Regenerate documentation after editing roxygen comments devtools::document()  # Full check before opening a PR devtools::check()

title: Contributing to estatr
text: Fork create feature branch. Add tests behaviour change; keep offline. Run devtools::check() make sure clean. Update NEWS.md user-facing summary.

title: Contributing to estatr
text: Please note project released Contributor Code Conduct. participating agree abide terms.

title: Understanding e-Stat's data model
text: table (statsDataId) set observations. Every observation single value tagged code several classification axes: tab — tabulated item (measured, e.g. “population”). cat01..cat15 — category axes (sex, age band, employment status, …). area — geography (national, prefecture, municipality, mesh). time — time period. raw data carries codes. metadata (getMetaInfo) maps code human label. estat_meta_info() returns one tibble per axis:
code: meta <- estat_meta_info("0003217721") names(meta)      # e.g. "tab" "cat01" "cat02" "cat03" "area" "time" meta$cat01       # code / name / level / parent / unit attr(meta, "axis_names") # the display name of each axis

title: Understanding e-Stat's data model
text: get_estat() hides split. fetches data (conveniently bundles metadata) performs code→label join , returning paired columns: Keep codes need stable joins; keep labels reading. want codes (skipping metadata join entirely), use fast path:
code: d <- get_estat("0003217721", limit = 100) # For each axis X you get X (label) and X_code (the raw code) names(d) get_estat("0003217721", decode_labels = FALSE, limit = 100)

title: Understanding e-Stat's data model
text: Category area codes often hierarchical: level column parent code let walk totals sub-categories. example, “ages” row (level 1) may parent individual age bands (level 2):
code: meta$cat02[, c("code", "name", "level", "parent")]

title: Understanding e-Stat's data model
text: single table can enormous (area-mesh data, long time series). e-Stat caps response 100,000 records; estatr pages rest automatically, fetching remaining pages concurrently absolute offset. large pulls, pass checkpoint file interrupted download resumes left :
code: big <- get_estat("0003217721", checkpoint = "lfs.checkpoint.rds")

title: Understanding e-Stat's data model
text: Two arguments skip work may need: decode_labels = FALSE — skip metadata join, return raw codes. as_data_table = TRUE — return internal data.table instead tibble, avoiding even boundary conversion bulk analysis. Metadata cached disk (see estat_cache_dir()), repeated calls table don’t refetch .

title: Getting started with estatr
text: e-Stat API requires free application ID (appId). Register https://www.e-stat.go.jp/api/ issue ID mypage (three allowed per account). Store estat_api_key(): key read ESTAT_API_KEY environment variable call time never stored package state printed. Treat secret: don’t commit paste issues. leaks, delete reissue mypage.
code: library(estatr)  # Session only estat_api_key("your-app-id")  # Or persist it to ~/.Renviron for future sessions estat_api_key("your-app-id", install = TRUE)

title: Getting started with estatr
text: estatr works English default — table names, category labels, area time labels, search come back English, e-Stat provides translations. handful tables English release fall back Japanese automatically (warning). work Japanese instead:
code: options(estatr.lang = "J") # for the whole session # ...or per call: get_estat(id, lang = "J")

title: Getting started with estatr
text: Every e-Stat table statsDataId. Search catalog search_estat() — English keywords work major surveys: Search coverage broadest Japanese (every table indexed English), search comes short, try Japanese terms curated shortcuts . just want common table without hunting, use curated shortcut:
code: tables <- search_estat("Labour Force Survey") tables[, c("id", "stat_name", "title")] estat_curated_tables() lfs <- get_labour_force_survey(limit = 500)

title: Getting started with estatr
text: get_estat() main entry point. Give statsDataId fetches data metadata one call, decodes every numeric code label, returns tidy tibble: get one row per observation, paired label/code columns classification axis (e.g. area + area_code, time + time_code), unit, numeric value, annotation column preserves non-numeric markers (suppressed cells, footnote symbols) instead silently turning NA. table’s annotation legend attached attribute:
code: d <- get_estat("0003217721", limit = 500) d attr(d, "notes")

title: Getting started with estatr
text: Pivot classification axis columns pivot_estat_wide(): Join English prefecture names using bundled prefectures table:
code: pivot_estat_wide(d, names_from = "cat01") library(dplyr) d |>   left_join(prefectures, by = "area_code")

title: Getting started with estatr
text: vignette("finding-tables") — searching curated shortcuts depth. vignette("time-series") — working time series across prefectures. vignette("data-model") — understanding e-Stat’s indicator / classification / area / time structure.

title: Getting started with estatr
text: Statistics retrieved e-Stat API provided Japan’s Statistics Bureau. Applications redistribute data must display credit line required e-Stat Terms Use. service uses e-Stat API content guaranteed government.

title: Finding the right table
text: handful tables people want first, named shortcut never touch statsDataId: Entries whose statsDataId NA recognised names yet curated specific table — use search () , open issue help curate .
code: estat_curated_tables()  get_labour_force_survey(limit = 500) get_family_income_survey(limit = 500)  # Or by key get_estat_curated("regional_statistics", limit = 500)

title: Finding the right table
text: search_estat() wraps catalog search returns tibble useful columns renamed stable snake_case (id, stat_name, title, gov_org, survey_date, …) moved front: id, inspect structure pulling data (see vignette("data-model")):
code: # Keyword search (Japanese or English terms) search_estat("国勢調査")  # Combine terms; restrict to recently updated tables search_estat("労働力調査 AND 都道府県", updated_from = "2023") meta <- estat_meta_info("0003217721") names(meta) # the classification axes meta$cat01 # the labels/codes for the first category axis

title: Finding the right table
text: full control raw getStatsList parameters, use estat_stats_list() directly:
code: estat_stats_list(   searchWord = "家計調査",   statsCode = "00200561",   limit = 20 )

title: Finding the right table
text: resources published Excel/CSV/PDF rather queryable data. estat_data_catalog() finds :
code: estat_data_catalog(searchWord = "国勢調査", limit = 10)

title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: Pass geometry = TRUE [get_estat()] get sf object back:
code: library(sf) library(ggplot2)  pop <- get_estat(   "0003433219",             # 2020 Population Census, population by sex   cdCat01 = "0",            # total (both sexes)   geometry = TRUE,   geometry_level = "prefecture",   geometry_year = 2020 )  ggplot(pop) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = value)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(trans = "log10") +   labs(title = "Population by prefecture, 2020", fill = "People")

what: Attaching geometry to data you already have
title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: already pulled data, join geometry separately [estat_join_geometry()]:
code: d <- get_estat("0003433219", cdCat01 = "0") sf_d <- estat_join_geometry(d, level = "prefecture", year = 2020)

title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: level (geometry_level) controls geography: "prefecture" — 47 prefectures (area_code ending 000). "municipality" — cities/wards/towns/villages (5-digit area_code). "small_area" — town-block (町丁・字), raw 9-digit KEY_CODE. "auto" — prefecture every area_code ends 000, else municipality. Boundaries downloaded per prefecture cached (see [estat_cache_dir()]), municipality map one prefecture fetches prefecture’s file.

what: Matching the boundary year to your data
title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: matters accuracy. Japanese municipality codes boundaries change censuses (mergers dissolutions), set geometry_year census year data. Joining 2020 data 2015 boundaries can silently drop mis-place municipalities changed .

title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: Boundaries come JGD2000 (datum = "2000", EPSG:4612, default) JGD2011 (datum = "2011", EPSG:6668). Reproject needed basemap, e.g. projected CRS area-correct rendering:
code: pop_proj <- sf::st_transform(pop, 6684) # JGD2011 / Japan Plane Rectangular CS

title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: ordinance-designated cities (政令指定都市) Tokyo, e-Stat’s boundary files use ward codes (e.g. Sapporo’s wards 01101–01110), parent-city code (01100) — e-Stat statistics report cities levels. default (designated_cities = ""), estatr returns ward polygons unioned parent-city polygon, data coded either way joins holes: Use designated_cities = "ward" wards , "city" replace designated city’s wards just parent-city polygon. argument available estat_join_geometry() geometry_designated_cities get_estat(). code still finds polygon triggers warning rather silent gap. Note also e-Stat’s disclaimer: boundaries drawn statistical tabulation necessarily coincide legal administrative borders.
code: # Sapporo appears as its 10 wards AND as 01100 札幌市 (union of the wards) bnd <- estat_boundaries("01", level = "municipality", year = 2020)

title: Mapping e-Stat data (choropleths)
text: Boundary data e-Stat (統計地理情報システム). Maps publish must carry e-Stat credit line; see e-Stat Terms Use.

title: Working with time series across prefectures
text: Filter data leaves e-Stat passing cd* codes get_estat(). far cheaper pulling everything filtering R: cdTime, cdArea, cdCat01..cdCat15 accept vectors, comma-joined e-Stat’s code-list form : bundled prefectures table codes:
code: # One category, a time window, all areas d <- get_estat(   "0003217721",   cdCat03 = "1",            # e.g. a single sex category   cdTimeFrom = "2015000000",   cdTimeTo = "2023000000" ) # Just Tokyo and Osaka (whole-prefecture area codes) get_estat("0003217721", cdArea = c("13000", "27000")) prefectures |>   filter(name_en %in% c("Tokyo", "Osaka")) |>   select(name_en, area_code)

title: Working with time series across prefectures
text: e-Stat time codes numeric (time_code) human label (time). plotting, derive proper date/period whichever convenient. Quarterly labour-force codes, example, encode year first four digits:
code: d <- d |>   mutate(year = as.integer(substr(time_code, 1, 4)))

title: Working with time series across prefectures
text: get_estat() returns tidy tibble numeric value, drops straight ggplot2:
code: d |>   filter(!is.na(value)) |>   ggplot(aes(year, value, colour = cat01)) +   geom_line() +   labs(y = unique(d$unit), colour = NULL)

title: Working with time series across prefectures
text: Non-numeric markers (suppressed cells, footnotes) silently coerced NA — preserved annotation column, legend attached attribute. Inspect dropping:
code: d |> filter(!is.na(annotation)) |> count(annotation) attr(d, "notes")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Sean Griffin. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Griffin S (2026). estatr: Tidy Access Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API). R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/smgriffin/estatr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {estatr: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)},   author = {Sean Griffin},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/smgriffin/estatr}, }

what: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
title: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
text: 📖 Documentation: https://smgriffin.github.io/estatr/ estatr tidy, tidycensus-style R interface Japanese government-wide statistics catalog served e-Stat API (api.e-stat.go.jp) — Population Census, Labour Force Survey, Economic Census, rest official catalog. wraps table search, classification metadata, data retrieval; decodes e-Stat’s numeric codes human-readable labels; returns tibbles pipe straight tidyverse. Internally uses data.table speed large tables, converting plain tibble return boundary. Status: development version, feature-complete first release. Search, metadata, data retrieval automatic parallel pagination, label decoding, caching, resumable pulls, choropleth-ready boundary geometry (geometry = TRUE, via suggested sf package) place.

title: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("smgriffin/estatr")

title: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
text: e-Stat API requires free appId. Sign https://www.e-stat.go.jp/api/ issue application ID, register : writes ESTAT_API_KEY .Renviron available future sessions. key secret: never commit paste issues.
code: library(estatr) estat_api_key("your-app-id", install = TRUE)

title: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
text: default estatr works English — labels, category names, search come back English (e-Stat provides translations; tables English release fall back Japanese automatically). Set options(estatr.lang = "J") pass lang = "J" Japanese. get_estat() returns one row per observation paired label/code columns, numeric value, annotation column preserving suppressed/footnoted markers. See vignette("estatr") get started.
code: library(estatr)  # 1. Find a table — English keywords work for the major surveys tables <- search_estat("Labour Force Survey")  # 2. Get tidy, labelled data in one call (data + metadata, decoded) d <- get_estat("0003217721", limit = 500) #> # A tibble: 500 × 9 #>   area      area_code time           time_code  cat01                        cat01_code unit          value annotation #>   <chr>     <chr>     <chr>          <chr>      <chr>                        <chr>      <chr>         <dbl> <chr> #> 1 All Japan 00000     Jan.-Mar. 2018 2018000103 Population aged 15 and over  00         10 thousand   11077 NA #> …  # 3. Or skip the id lookup with a curated shortcut lfs <- get_labour_force_survey(limit = 500)  # 4. Map it: get an sf object with official e-Stat boundaries joined on library(sf) pop <- get_estat("0003433219", cdCat01 = "0", geometry = TRUE,                  geometry_level = "prefecture", geometry_year = 2020)

title: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
text: Statistics retrieved e-Stat API provided Japan’s Statistics Bureau / Ministry Internal Affairs Communications. Applications redistribute data must display credit line required e-Stat Terms Use. service uses API function government statistics portal site (e-Stat) content guaranteed government.

title: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API)
text: MIT © estatr authors

what: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
title: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
text: Registers e-Stat appId current session, optionally writes .Renviron available future sessions. e-Stat API requires free appId; obtain one https://www.e-stat.go.jp/api/.

title: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
code: estat_api_key(key, install = FALSE, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
text: key e-Stat appId, string. install TRUE, write key .Renviron persists across sessions. Defaults FALSE (session ). overwrite TRUE, replace existing ESTAT_API_KEY entry .Renviron. Ignored install = FALSE.

title: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
text: Invisibly, key (can piped), though never printed.

title: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
text: key secret. function never prints , never commit version control paste issues. key ever exposed, delete reissue e-Stat mypage (3 allowed per account).

title: Set your e-Stat API key — estat_api_key
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Session only estat_api_key("your-app-id")  # Persist for future sessions estat_api_key("your-app-id", install = TRUE) } # }

what: Is an e-Stat API key available? — estat_api_key_exists
title: Is an e-Stat API key available? — estat_api_key_exists
text: e-Stat API key available?

title: Is an e-Stat API key available? — estat_api_key_exists
code: estat_api_key_exists()

title: Is an e-Stat API key available? — estat_api_key_exists
text: TRUE non-empty ESTAT_API_KEY set, otherwise FALSE.

what: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
title: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
text: Fetches official e-Stat census boundary polygons returns sf object area_code column matching codes used get_estat(), two join cleanly. Boundaries derived e-Stat's authoritative small-area (町丁・字) shapefiles dissolved requested level.

title: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
code: estat_boundaries(   areas = NULL,   level = c("municipality", "prefecture", "small_area"),   year = 2020,   datum = c("2000", "2011"),   cache = TRUE,   designated_cities = c("both", "ward", "city") )

title: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
text: areas Character vector area codes selecting prefectures download: 2-digit prefecture codes (e.g. "31") codes whose first two digits prefecture (e.g. 5-digit "31201" area_code column get_estat()). NULL downloads 47 prefectures (large). level Geographic level return: "municipality" (default; 5-digit PREF+CITY) "prefecture" (dissolved whole prefectures, PREF+"000") "small_area" (raw 町丁・字, 9-digit KEY_CODE). year Census year boundaries (e.g. 2020, 2015). Match census year data: municipality codes boundaries change censuses (mergers), mismatched year can mis-join. datum Geodetic datum: "2000" (JGD2000, EPSG:4612, default) "2011" (JGD2011, EPSG:6668). cache TRUE (default), cache downloaded boundary files estat_cache_dir(). designated_cities handle 20 ordinance-designated cities Tokyo's special wards level = "municipality", since e-Stat's shapefiles carry ward codes: "" (default) returns ward polygons unioned parent-city polygon (e.g. 01101… 01100 札幌市), data coded either level joins; "ward" returns wards ; "city" returns parent city . Ignored levels.

title: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
text: sf object area_code, name columns, geometry.

title: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
text: Requires sf package. Downloaded files cached (see estat_cache_dir()).

title: Download e-Stat administrative boundaries as an sf object — estat_boundaries
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Municipalities of Tottori (prefecture 31), 2020 census boundaries bnd <- estat_boundaries("31", level = "municipality", year = 2020) } # }

title: Clear the estatr cache — estat_cache_clear
text: Removes cached metadata /pagination checkpoints estat_cache_dir().

title: Clear the estatr cache — estat_cache_clear
code: estat_cache_clear(what = c("all", "meta", "checkpoints"))

title: Clear the estatr cache — estat_cache_clear
text: caches clear: "meta", "checkpoints", "" (default).

title: Clear the estatr cache — estat_cache_clear
text: Invisibly, number files removed.

title: Clear the estatr cache — estat_cache_clear
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ estat_cache_clear() } # }

what: Location of the estatr cache — estat_cache_dir
title: Location of the estatr cache — estat_cache_dir
text: Returns directory estatr uses cached metadata pagination checkpoints. Override options(estatr.cache_dir = "...").

title: Location of the estatr cache — estat_cache_dir
code: estat_cache_dir()

title: Location of the estatr cache — estat_cache_dir
text: file path (character scalar). directory created demand.

what: List the curated e-Stat shortcut tables — estat_curated_tables
title: List the curated e-Stat shortcut tables — estat_curated_tables
text: Returns built-table curated shortcuts used get_estat_curated() survey-specific helpers. Entries whose statsDataId NA recognised names yet curated specific table.

title: List the curated e-Stat shortcut tables — estat_curated_tables
code: estat_curated_tables()

title: List the curated e-Stat shortcut tables — estat_curated_tables
text: tibble key, statsDataId, label_en, label_ja columns.

title: List the curated e-Stat shortcut tables — estat_curated_tables
code: estat_curated_tables() #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   key                  statsDataId label_en                             label_ja #>   <chr>                <chr>       <chr>                                <chr>    #> 1 labour_force_survey  0003005798  Labour Force Survey: population by … 労働力調査 就… #> 2 family_income_survey 0002070001  Family Income and Expenditure Survey 家計調査 家計… #> 3 regional_statistics  0000010106  Social & demographic statistics by … 社会・人口統計… #> 4 population_census    0003433219  Population Census 2020: population … 令和2年国勢調… #> 5 economic_census      0004005652  Economic Census 2021: establishment… 令和3年経済セ…

what: Search the e-Stat data catalog for datasets and files — estat_data_catalog
title: Search the e-Stat data catalog for datasets and files — estat_data_catalog
text: Wraps e-Stat getDataCatalog endpoint, returns catalog entries — datasets downloadable resources (Excel/CSV/PDF URLs) — rather machine-readable data values. Use estat_stats_data() / get_estat() actual numbers.

title: Search the e-Stat data catalog for datasets and files — estat_data_catalog
code: estat_data_catalog(   searchWord = NULL,   ...,   lang = getOption("estatr.lang", "E"),   key = get_estat_key() )

title: Search the e-Stat data catalog for datasets and files — estat_data_catalog
text: searchWord Keyword(s) search. Japanese supported. ... query parameters passed getDataCatalog verbatim (e.g. statsCode, dataType, limit, startPosition). lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key.

title: Search the e-Stat data catalog for datasets and files — estat_data_catalog
text: tibble catalog entries, one row per entry. Returns zero-row tibble nothing matches.

title: Search the e-Stat data catalog for datasets and files — estat_data_catalog
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ estat_data_catalog(searchWord = "国勢調査", limit = 10) } # }

what: Attach boundary geometry to e-Stat data — estat_join_geometry
title: Attach boundary geometry to e-Stat data — estat_join_geometry
text: Joins e-Stat boundary polygons onto tidy get_estat() result area_code, returning sf object ready choropleth mapping. data row receives area's geometry (long time series repeats geometry per period, matching tidycensus long-plus-geometry convention).

title: Attach boundary geometry to e-Stat data — estat_join_geometry
code: estat_join_geometry(   data,   level = c("auto", "municipality", "prefecture", "small_area"),   year = 2020,   datum = c("2000", "2011"),   designated_cities = c("both", "ward", "city") )

title: Attach boundary geometry to e-Stat data — estat_join_geometry
text: data tibble get_estat() (decoded, area_code column). level Geographic level, "auto" (default) infer: "prefecture" every area_code ends "000", otherwise "municipality". year, datum, designated_cities Passed estat_boundaries(). Match year data's census year. designated_cities defaults "" data coded either ward parent-city level joins.

title: Attach boundary geometry to e-Stat data — estat_join_geometry
text: sf object: input columns plus geometry column.

title: Attach boundary geometry to e-Stat data — estat_join_geometry
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ d <- get_population_census(cdCat01 = "0") sf_d <- estat_join_geometry(d, level = "prefecture", year = 2020) } # }

what: Retrieve classification metadata for an e-Stat table — estat_meta_info
title: Retrieve classification metadata for an e-Stat table — estat_meta_info
text: Wraps e-Stat getMetaInfo endpoint. Returns classification metadata needed decode table's numeric codes labels: one tibble per classification axis (tab, cat01, ..., area, time), keyed axis id, code, name, level, unit, parent columns.

title: Retrieve classification metadata for an e-Stat table — estat_meta_info
code: estat_meta_info(   statsDataId,   lang = getOption("estatr.lang", "E"),   key = get_estat_key(),   cache = TRUE,   cache_ttl = getOption("estatr.cache_ttl", 30 * 24 * 3600) )

title: Retrieve classification metadata for an e-Stat table — estat_meta_info
text: statsDataId table id whose metadata fetch. lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key. cache TRUE (default), read/write parsed metadata -disk cache (see estat_cache_dir()); metadata rarely changes, avoids network round-trip repeat calls. Set FALSE force fetch. cache_ttl Maximum age, seconds, cached entry refetched. Defaults options(estatr.cache_ttl) 30 days.

title: Retrieve classification metadata for an e-Stat table — estat_meta_info
text: named list tibbles, one per classification axis, plus table_info attribute table's overall description.

title: Retrieve classification metadata for an e-Stat table — estat_meta_info
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ meta <- estat_meta_info("0003217721") meta$cat01 # labels for the first category axis } # }

what: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
title: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
text: Low-level wrapper around e-Stat getStatsData endpoint. Given statsDataId (estat_stats_list()) optional filter codes, returns tibble raw data values, one row per observation, e-Stat's numeric classification codes intact (tab, cat01, ..., area, time, unit, value).

title: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
code: estat_stats_data(   statsDataId,   ...,   limit = NULL,   start_position = 1L,   checkpoint = NULL,   lang = getOption("estatr.lang", "E"),   key = get_estat_key() )

title: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
text: statsDataId table id retrieve (~10-digit string). ... Filter parameters passed getStatsData, e.g. cdCat01, cdArea, cdTime, cdTimeFrom, cdTimeTo. Vectors comma-joined e-Stat's expected code-list form. limit Maximum number rows return. NULL (default) returns matching rows, paginating needed. start_position 1-based absolute row offset start . checkpoint Optional path checkpoint file resumable pulls. set, page's rows persisted keyed absolute offset, interrupted large pull resumes re-requesting missing pages. lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key.

title: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
text: tibble coded data values.

title: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
text: Pagination automatic: e-Stat caps response 100,000 records, function fetches remaining pages concurrently (bounded, throttled) using total record count reported first page. decoded, analysis-ready output human-readable labels, use get_estat().

title: Retrieve statistical data values from e-Stat — estat_stats_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ estat_api_key("your-app-id") estat_stats_data("0003217721", cdCat03 = "1", limit = 100) } # }

what: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
title: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
text: Low-level wrapper around e-Stat getStatsList endpoint. Searches government-wide statistics catalog returns tibble matching tables, one row per table, including id (statsDataId pass estat_stats_data() wrapper implemented).

title: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
code: estat_stats_list(   searchWord = NULL,   statsCode = NULL,   surveyYears = NULL,   statsField = NULL,   searchKind = NULL,   limit = NULL,   startPosition = NULL,   ...,   lang = getOption("estatr.lang", "E"),   key = get_estat_key() )

title: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
text: searchWord Keyword(s) search. Japanese supported encoded UTF-8. Combine terms /per e-Stat API. statsCode Government statistics code filter (5 8 digits). surveyYears Survey period filter: yyyy, yyyymm, yyyymm-yyyymm. statsField Statistics field code (2 4 digits). searchKind Data kind: 1 (statistics, default) 2 (regional statistics / sub-datasets). limit Maximum number tables return. startPosition 1-based row offset start . ... query parameters passed getStatsList verbatim (e.g. updatedDate, openYears). lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key.

title: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
text: tibble one row per matching table. Returns zero-row tibble search matches nothing.

title: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
text: power-user function mirrors API closely. Higher-level, friendlier search (search_estat()) planned later milestone.

title: Search the e-Stat catalog for statistical tables — estat_stats_list
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ estat_api_key("your-app-id") # Tables mentioning the Labour Force Survey estat_stats_list(searchWord = "労働力調査") } # }

what: estatr: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API) — estatr-package
title: estatr: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API) — estatr-package
text: tidy, tidycensus-style interface Japanese government-wide statistics catalog served e-Stat API (https://www.e-stat.go.jp/api/). Wraps table search, classification metadata, data retrieval, decoding e-Stat's numeric codes human-readable labels returning tibbles pipe tidyverse. Internals use 'data.table' speed large tables; output plain tibble return boundary.

title: estatr: Tidy Access to Japanese Government Statistics (e-Stat API) — estatr-package
text: Maintainer: Sean Griffin bantam-lists-79@icloud.com

what: Get the Economic Census (establishments by industry) — get_economic_census
title: Get the Economic Census (establishments by industry) — get_economic_census
text: Convenience wrapper 2021 Economic Census (activity survey) table establishment counts industry. Equivalent get_estat_curated("economic_census", ...).

title: Get the Economic Census (establishments by industry) — get_economic_census
code: get_economic_census(...)

title: Get the Economic Census (establishments by industry) — get_economic_census
text: ... Arguments passed get_estat decode_labels TRUE (default), join metadata labels onto coded values. FALSE skips metadata join entirely returns just coded columns — power-user fast path. as_data_table TRUE, return internal data.table directly instead converting tibble, bulk-analysis users want even boundary conversion. Defaults FALSE. limit Maximum number rows return. NULL (default) returns matching rows. checkpoint Optional path checkpoint file resumable pulls (see estat_stats_data()). geometry TRUE, join e-Stat boundary polygons onto result area_code return sf object choropleth mapping (see estat_join_geometry()). Requires sf package decode_labels = TRUE. Defaults FALSE. geometry_level,geometry_year,geometry_datum,geometry_designated_cities Passed estat_join_geometry() geometry = TRUE. Match geometry_year census year data. lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key.

title: Get the Economic Census (establishments by industry) — get_economic_census
text: tidy tibble get_estat().

title: Get the Economic Census (establishments by industry) — get_economic_census
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ get_economic_census(limit = 500) } # }

what: Get tidy, labelled data from e-Stat — get_estat
title: Get tidy, labelled data from e-Stat — get_estat
text: main entry point. Given statsDataId, fetches data classification metadata one call, decodes every numeric code human-readable label, returns tidy tibble: one row per observation paired label/code columns classification axis (e.g. area + area_code, time + time_code), unit, numeric value, annotation column preserving non-numeric markers (suppressed cells, footnote symbols) instead silently coercing NA.

title: Get tidy, labelled data from e-Stat — get_estat
code: get_estat(   statsDataId,   ...,   decode_labels = TRUE,   as_data_table = FALSE,   limit = NULL,   checkpoint = NULL,   geometry = FALSE,   geometry_level = "auto",   geometry_year = 2020,   geometry_datum = "2000",   geometry_designated_cities = "both",   lang = getOption("estatr.lang", "E"),   key = get_estat_key() )

title: Get tidy, labelled data from e-Stat — get_estat
text: statsDataId table id retrieve (estat_stats_list() search_estat()). ... Filter parameters passed getStatsData (e.g. cdCat01, cdArea, cdTime, cdTimeFrom, cdTimeTo). decode_labels TRUE (default), join metadata labels onto coded values. FALSE skips metadata join entirely returns just coded columns — power-user fast path. as_data_table TRUE, return internal data.table directly instead converting tibble, bulk-analysis users want even boundary conversion. Defaults FALSE. limit Maximum number rows return. NULL (default) returns matching rows. checkpoint Optional path checkpoint file resumable pulls (see estat_stats_data()). geometry TRUE, join e-Stat boundary polygons onto result area_code return sf object choropleth mapping (see estat_join_geometry()). Requires sf package decode_labels = TRUE. Defaults FALSE. geometry_level, geometry_year, geometry_datum, geometry_designated_cities Passed estat_join_geometry() geometry = TRUE. Match geometry_year census year data. lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key.

title: Get tidy, labelled data from e-Stat — get_estat
text: tidy tibble (data.table as_data_table, sf object geometry = TRUE), notes attribute holding table's annotation legend.

title: Get tidy, labelled data from e-Stat — get_estat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ estat_api_key("your-app-id") # Labour Force Survey, one category, decoded to labels get_estat("0003217721", cdCat03 = "1", limit = 500) } # }

what: Pivot tidy e-Stat output to wide form — pivot_estat_wide
title: Pivot tidy e-Stat output to wide form — pivot_estat_wide
text: Reshapes long/tidy output get_estat() wide form, spreading labels one classification axis across columns. Mirrors tidycensus's output = "wide", explicit opt-step rather default.

title: Pivot tidy e-Stat output to wide form — pivot_estat_wide
code: pivot_estat_wide(data, names_from, values_from = "value", id_cols = NULL)

title: Pivot tidy e-Stat output to wide form — pivot_estat_wide
text: data tibble data.frame get_estat() (decode_labels). names_from Column whose values become new column names (e.g. "cat01"). paired _code column dropped id set. values_from Column holding cell values spread. Defaults "value". id_cols Columns identifying output row. Defaults every column except names_from (_code partner), values_from, annotation.

title: Pivot tidy e-Stat output to wide form — pivot_estat_wide
text: wide tibble, one row per unique combination id_cols.

title: Pivot tidy e-Stat output to wide form — pivot_estat_wide
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ d <- get_estat("0003217721", limit = 500) pivot_estat_wide(d, names_from = "cat01") } # }

what: Japanese prefectures with JIS codes and e-Stat area codes — prefectures
title: Japanese prefectures with JIS codes and e-Stat area codes — prefectures
text: 47 Japanese prefectures (都道府県) JIS X 0401 codes English/Japanese names, joining filtering e-Stat area codes without another API round-trip. e-Stat whole-prefecture area code 2-digit JIS code followed "000" (e.g. Tokyo = "13000").

title: Japanese prefectures with JIS codes and e-Stat area codes — prefectures
code: prefectures

title: Japanese prefectures with JIS codes and e-Stat area codes — prefectures
text: tibble 47 rows 5 columns: code 2-digit JIS X 0401 prefecture code, e.g. "13". area_code 5-digit e-Stat area code whole prefecture, e.g. "13000". name_en English (romaji) prefecture name, e.g. "Tokyo". name_ja Japanese prefecture name, e.g. "東京都". region_en English region name, e.g. "Kanto".

title: Japanese prefectures with JIS codes and e-Stat area codes — prefectures
text: JIS X 0401 (Japanese Industrial Standard) prefecture codes.

title: Japanese prefectures with JIS codes and e-Stat area codes — prefectures
code: head(prefectures) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   code  area_code name_en  name_ja region_en #>   <chr> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>   <chr>     #> 1 01    01000     Hokkaido 北海道  Hokkaido  #> 2 02    02000     Aomori   青森県  Tohoku    #> 3 03    03000     Iwate    岩手県  Tohoku    #> 4 04    04000     Miyagi   宮城県  Tohoku    #> 5 05    05000     Akita    秋田県  Tohoku    #> 6 06    06000     Yamagata 山形県  Tohoku    # Join onto a get_estat() result by area_code to add English names

title: Search the e-Stat catalog (interactive-friendly) — search_estat
text: friendlier wrapper estat_stats_list() finding tables interactively. Returns tibble useful columns renamed stable snake_case (id, stat_name, title, gov_org, survey_date, ...) moved front, raw columns kept .

title: Search the e-Stat catalog (interactive-friendly) — search_estat
code: search_estat(   keyword = NULL,   gov_org = NULL,   updated_from = NULL,   updated_to = NULL,   limit = 100L,   ...,   lang = getOption("estatr.lang", "E"),   key = get_estat_key() )

title: Search the e-Stat catalog (interactive-friendly) — search_estat
text: keyword Keyword(s) search . Japanese supported. Combine terms /per e-Stat API. gov_org Government organisation code filter (passed statsCode's org prefix assumed; use estat_stats_list() full control). updated_from, updated_to Optional update-date bounds (yyyymmdd yyyymm) restrict recently refreshed tables. limit Maximum number tables return (default 100). ... parameters passed estat_stats_list(). lang Label language: "E" English (package default, settable options(estatr.lang = )) "J" Japanese. Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning. key e-Stat appId. Defaults stored key.

title: Search the e-Stat catalog (interactive-friendly) — search_estat
text: tibble matching tables, friendly columns first.

title: Search the e-Stat catalog (interactive-friendly) — search_estat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ search_estat("労働力調査") search_estat("国勢調査", updated_from = "2020") } # }

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: First development release: tidy, tidycensus-style interface Japanese e-Stat API, covering roadmap milestones M0–M6.

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: Works English default: table names, category/area/time labels, search return English (e-Stat provides translations). Control per call lang = "E" / "J", globally options(estatr.lang = ). Tables English release fall back Japanese automatically, warning, English mode never errors returns blanks.

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: get_estat(): main entry point. Fetches data classification metadata single call, decodes every numeric code label via data.table binary joins, returns tidy tibble paired label/code columns (e.g. area + area_code, time + time_code), unit, numeric value, annotation column preserves non-numeric markers (suppressed cells, footnotes) instead coercing silently NA. table’s annotation legend attached notes attribute. Fast paths: decode_labels = FALSE as_data_table = TRUE. pivot_estat_wide(): reshape tidy output wide form (data.table::dcast).

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: search_estat(): friendly catalog search stable snake_case columns. Curated shortcuts — estat_curated_tables(), get_estat_curated(), get_labour_force_survey(), get_family_income_survey(), get_population_census(), get_economic_census() — common tables need statsDataId lookup. prefectures: bundled reference data (47 prefectures, JIS + e-Stat area codes, English/Japanese names).

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: estat_stats_list() (getStatsList), estat_meta_info() (getMetaInfo), estat_stats_data() (getStatsData), estat_data_catalog() (getDataCatalog). estat_stats_data() paginates automatically, fetching pages beyond 100,000-record cap concurrently absolute offset (“parallel polite”, bounded throttled) rather leaving pagination user.

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: estat_api_key() / estat_api_key_exists(): key management via environment variable optional .Renviron; key never stored package state printed, redacted every error message, URL, fixture. Robust HTTP layer: JSON endpoints default, UTF-8 query encoding, gzip, transient-retry, client-side throttling. e-Stat’s RESULT.STATUS treated source truth, errors mapped classed conditions (estat_error_invalid_param, estat_error_no_data, …). Caching: -disk metadata cache TTL (estat_cache_dir(), estat_cache_clear()) layered -session memoisation. Resumable pulls: pass checkpoint = get_estat() / estat_stats_data() persist completed page offsets, interrupted large pull resumes re-requesting missing pages.

title: estatr 0.0.0.9000
text: get_estat(geometry = TRUE) returns sf object official e-Stat boundary polygons joined area_code, ready choropleth mapping. estat_boundaries() downloads dissolves e-Stat census boundaries prefecture, municipality, small-area (町丁・字) level; estat_join_geometry() attaches existing get_estat() result. Boundaries cached, read correct Shift-JIS encoding JGD2000/2011 CRS, repaired st_make_valid(). Requires suggested  package. Match boundary year data’s census year, since municipality codes change censuses. Designated cities (政令指定都市) Tokyo’s special wards: e-Stat’s shapefiles carry ward codes, statistics report ward parent-city level. designated_cities = "" (default) returns ward polygons unioned parent-city polygon (e.g. 01101… 01100 札幌市), data coded either level joins without holes; "ward" "city" select one .
